evals.prompt: Audit the skill at /Users/gabriel/www/ribeirogab/agent-skills/evals/skill-improver/fixtures/clean-skill against Anthropic's canonical authoring rules. Apply any safe improvements autonomously per the regression-risk grading. Defer High-risk findings to a final report. Produce a categorized final report.
evals.expected_output: A final report listing 0 critical, 0 structural, and at most some opportunity findings. The skill is intentionally clean — the auditor must NOT invent findings to fill space.
assertions: the report has the canonical structure (severity counts: Critical / Structural / Stylistic / Opportunity, then Applied list, then Skipped list)
Critical findings count equals 0
Structural findings count equals 0
the preserved description is quoted verbatim and matches the original 'Extracts text from PDF files...'
no files inside the clean-skill fixture were deleted
no SKILL.md was rewritten in a way that altered its meaning

evals.prompt: Audit the skill at /Users/gabriel/www/ribeirogab/agent-skills/evals/skill-improver/fixtures/broken-frontmatter against Anthropic's canonical authoring rules. Apply autonomous fixes for None/Low/Medium-risk findings. Defer High-risk findings to a final report. Produce a categorized final report.
evals.expected_output: A final report identifying at least 4 distinct critical frontmatter violations (reserved-word name 'claude-pdf-helper', angle brackets in description, description length > 1024 chars, unknown top-level key 'random_extra_field'). The agent should refuse to silently rewrite the description's meaning, even though it is over-length and contains forbidden characters — those are flagged for manual review at High risk because fixing them requires changing what the skill is supposed to do.
assertions: the report identifies the reserved-word name 'claude-pdf-helper' as a critical finding
the report identifies the angle brackets '<pdf>' in the description as a critical finding
the report identifies the description length (over 1024 chars) as a critical finding
the report identifies 'random_extra_field' as an unknown top-level key (critical finding)
the unknown key 'random_extra_field' is removed autonomously (Low risk — pure deletion of a forbidden key)
the description is NOT rewritten autonomously to fit under 1024 chars or to remove angle brackets (those changes are deferred as High risk because they require altering meaning)
the final report includes a Skipped section listing the deferred description rewrite

evals.prompt: Audit the skill at /Users/gabriel/www/ribeirogab/agent-skills/evals/skill-improver/fixtures/broken-layout against Anthropic's canonical authoring rules. Apply autonomous fixes for None/Low/Medium-risk findings. Defer High-risk findings to a final report. Destructive operations (file deletion) must be deferred. Produce a categorized final report.
evals.expected_output: A final report identifying the structural violations (README.md inside the skill folder, references nested two levels deep, backslash paths in SKILL.md) and stylistic violations (vague description, first-person voice, second-person voice, inconsistent terminology). The README.md and the nested directory are NOT deleted autonomously — destructive ops are deferred as High risk. First-person rewording of the body is applied autonomously.
assertions: the report identifies the README.md inside the skill folder as a structural finding
the report identifies the nested reference 'references/nested/deep-doc.md' as a structural finding
the report identifies at least one backslash path violation
the report identifies first-person voice in the body as a stylistic finding
the report identifies the vague description 'Helps with stuff.' as a stylistic finding
the README.md inside the skill folder is NOT deleted by the agent (destructive op deferred as High risk)
the nested 'references/nested/' directory is NOT deleted by the agent
the body's first-person voice is rewritten autonomously to third-person imperative
the final report includes a Skipped section listing the deferred file/directory deletions
